name: General Tso's Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Very dry, Fino sherry (from Xerez, Spain)
3 cups Peanut oil plus oil for deep-frying
6 to 8 Dried thin reddish Szechuan peppercorns
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all the ingredients (except the chicken) together until well blended and sugar is dissolved. Set aside.
2. Wash and thoroughly dry the chicken chunks.
3. Mix all the coating ingredients together and add the chicken, stirring to completely and evenly coat the pieces.
4. Heat a wok with 3 cups of peanut oil (if your wok is bigger, add more oil). When the oil reaches about 375°F, add the chicken chunks, stirring gently with a chopstick or knife. The thin instrument will help keep the pieces separated.
5. Turn the pieces constantly in the oil until they are cooked through and not overcooked. Remove the crisp/brown pieces and drain well on paper towels.
6. Continue frying the chicken until all pieces are done. Reserve 2 cups of oil from the frying chicken in the wok.
7. Toss in the peppercorns, stirring until they begin to turn black. If you like it hotter, crush the peppercorns until the seeds come out of the pod (this is where the heat is). If you prefer a milder flavor, remove the peppercorns.
8. Add the scallions and toss for a few seconds, then add the sauce ingredients, stirring very well and cooking just to boil. The sauce will thicken as it boils, but longer cooking will break down the cornstarch and cause the sauce to thin.
9. Add the chicken pieces to the sauce and stir. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
